title: Symmetry discovery vs hard-negative mining for generative models (EqM)
research_question: Can a learned manifold-preserving operator, anchored to data (not the live field), discover a nonlinearly-hidden symmetry and fill held-out manifold regions? vs hard-negative mining and known-symmetry constraints.
scale: CPU toy (2D/16D rings). NOT publishable; filters/diagnostics only.

ladder_status.rung1_arithmetic: equivariance constraint perfect; hard-neg mining dead
ladder_status.rung2_ring_linear: known + discovered rotation both work (symmetry linear in observed)
ladder_status.rung5_oracle_latent_control: recipe collapses M->identity even with perfect latent (recipe bug exposed)
ladder_status.rung8_identity_exclusion: FAIL (M drifts to non-symmetry; closure can't anchor)
ladder_status.rung9_frozen_anchor: PARTIAL: frozen anchor FIXES field co-adaptation (T_onman 0.00->0.70) but distributional anchor under-constrains operator -> random on-manifold shuffle (shift_std ~95deg), arc not filled. FROZEN~=VISIBLE (no symmetry learned).
ladder_status.rung10_frozen_anchor_coherent: PARTIAL SUCCESS (best yet): single latent matrix fixes coherence (shift_std 95->25), recovers real rotation (M_angle ~-39deg), recall floor0.002->0.025 (12x floor, ~37% of ORACLE 0.068). All 3 blockers (identity/co-adaptation/coherence) now individually fixed. Remaining gap = single-generator vs full-group COVERAGE + imperfect latent + seed variance.
ladder_status.rung11_orbit_augmentation: Coverage hypothesis REJECTED: orbit aug == single aug (recall 0.024 both). Gap to ORACLE is NOT coverage but operator PRECISION (discovered M is fuzzy/seed-variable rotation, shift_std 25, M_off 1.04). FINAL: discovery possible-in-principle but imprecise -> below known-symmetry quality.
ladder_status.rung12_group_generator: SUCCESS - near-oracle discovery. Group-structured generator M=exp(theta*A) fixes operator precision: shift_std 25->0.8(skew)/7.6(free); M_det 0.56->1.00(skew)/0.90(free); recall 0.024->0.071(SKEW = ORACLE 0.067)/0.046(FREE ~69% ORACLE). SKEW recovers exact rotation (~3deg/seed). Gap was PRECISION not impossibility.
ladder_status.rung13_prior_budget: STRONG SUCCESS: GEN_FREE_STABLE (det~1 + well-conditioned, NO skew/rotation naming) = 99% ORACLE (recall 0.067, det 1.00, shift_std 0.84, angle within 2.8deg) -- MATCHES GEN_SKEW. Rotation EMERGES from stability prior; not needed to name it. Active ingredient = condition-number penalty (FREE w/o it stalls 69%). CAVEAT: 2D conflates stable~=rotation; definitive test needs higher-dim latent (rung14).
ladder_status.rung14_higher_dim: DEFINITIVE SUCCESS: K=3 cylinder (rot in plane, h passive, stable!=rotation). GEN_FREE_STABLE (general 3x3, det~1+cond->1, NO skew/plane) = 113% ORACLE, MATCHES both skew arms. Discovered rotation CONFINED to active plane (passive_leak 0.04~=0) + h PRESERVED (passive_dh 0.15 = plane-told ref) + exact group (det 1.00 cond 1.00). 2D-conflation caveat DISSOLVED.
ladder_status.rung15_learned_latent: PARTIAL: learned/recon latent reaches 89-97% recall BUT via COVERAGE not coherent symmetry (shift_std 34-69 vs supervised 3.4; passive_dh 0.96 vs 0.15 -> doesn't preserve true h). Stable on-manifold reshuffle covers 2D cylinder incl arc -> fills via coverage. recall is coverage-confounded; operator-quality metrics honest. Coherent discovery needs structure-ALIGNED coords; recon-only (0.196) faithful but not aligned. Open: symmetry-aware unsupervised latent (deferred, new mechanism).

key_findings: Hard-negative mining (v10 mechanism) installs no manifold structure in any rung. Dead.

UNSUPERVISED latent-symmetry discovery reaches ORACLE with a CLASS-AGNOSTIC prior: frozen anchor (rung9) + coherent operator (rung10) + group generator exp(A) (rung12) + stability-only reg det~1+cond->1 (rung13-14). NO rotation/plane/class naming needed.
Rung14 (K=3, stable!=rotation): stability prior discovers the correct active subspace + preserves passive dim, at oracle level -> 2D caveat dissolved.
Open: learned (not supervised) latent, non-abelian groups, real high-dim EqM scale.
Methodology: positive+negative controls in every run isolated each root cause.

next_action.reason: learned-latent coherence deferred (new mechanism); isolate group-complexity axis with clean latent.
next_action.hint: rung16: screw/helix z(phi)=[r cosphi, r sinphi, c phi], symmetry phi->phi+d & height->height+c*d (rotation+translation coupled). Arms BASE/ORACLE/GEN_FREE_STABLE (+strong ref). Stable generator must learn coupled motion. Then EqM bridge v12.

completed_runs.result: PARTIAL: recall transfers via coverage, coherent symmetry needs aligned coords
DEFINITIVE SUCCESS: stability prior = oracle in K=3 (stable!=rotation), discovers active plane + preserves passive dim
STRONG SUCCESS: stability-only prior = 99% ORACLE (rotation emerges, not named)
PARTIAL SUCCESS (coherence fixed, partial arc fill)

events.detail: Project created from diff-EqM symmetry-toys investigation (rungs 1-9). Houses ladder scripts, findings, control methodology.
Coherent single-matrix operator + frozen anchor -> partial discovery success; 3-layer blocker resolved in principle.
Orbit aug rejected coverage hypothesis; gap is operator precision. Ladder concluded: discovery partial+imprecise, known-symmetry remains best lever.
Group-structured generator achieves near-oracle latent symmetry discovery; precision was the gap. Verdict revised: discovery VIABLE.
Prior-budget: stability-only prior (no rotation naming) reaches oracle (99%); rotation emerges. 2D-conflation caveat -> rung14 higher-dim definitive test.
Class-agnostic stability prior reaches oracle in K=3 (stable!=rotation); discovers active subspace + preserves passive dim. 2D caveat dissolved. Discovery viable with minimal prior.
Learned latent: recall via coverage, not coherent symmetry. Coherent discovery needs structure-aligned coords. Deferred learned-latent coherence; proceeding to rung16 richer group with clean latent.

provenance.informs: diff-EqM v10 direction (prefer known-symmetry constraints over hard-negative mining)
